title: Scrubber wash water electrolytic treatment
abstract: The invention provides a method and apparatus for treating waste scrubber liquid from a wet scrubber. The method comprises subjecting the waste scrubber liquid to electrolysis, thereby providing concentrated waste scrubber liquid and at least one fuel, which is typically hydrogen. A power plant, waste disposal facility, ship or vehicle comprising the apparatus or using the method of the invention is also provided.
claims: Claims I. A method for treating waste scrubber liquid from a wet scrubber, said method comprising the step of subjecting said waste scrubber liquid to electrolysis, thereby providing concentrated waste scrubber liquid and at least one fuel. 2. The method according to claim 1, wherein said fuel is hydrogen or a biofuel, preferably hydrogen. 3. The method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the fuel from the electrolysis is collected in one or more fuel storage containers. 4. The method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein said electrolysis also provides oxygen. 5. The method according to claim 4, wherein the oxygen from the electrolysis is collected in one or more oxygen storage containers. 6. The method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein said electrolysis step takes place in an electrolytic cell. 7. The method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein one or more additives are introduced to the waste scrubber liquid, before or during electrolysis. 8. The method according to claim 7, wherein said one or more additives is ammonia. 9. The method according to any one of the preceding claims, further comprising a step of producing electricity from said fuel. 10. The method according to claim 9, wherein the electricity produced from said fuel is used to power the electrolysis process. II. The method according to any one of claims 9 or 10, wherein a fuel cell is used to produce said electricity from said fuel. 12. The method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the waste scrubber liquid originates from seawater. 13. An apparatus for treating waste scrubber liquid, said apparatus comprising; a. a wet scrubber; b. an electrolytic cell arranged so as to receive waste scrubber liquid from said wet scrubber and to subject it to electrolysis, thereby providing concentrated waste scrubber liquid and at least one fuel. 14. The apparatus according to claim 13 additionally comprising at least one fuel storage container arranged to collect said at least one fuel from said electrolytic cell. 15. The apparatus according to any one of claims 13-14, further comprising means for receiving said at least one fuel from said electrolytic cell or said fuel storage container and producing electricity from said fuel. 16. The apparatus according to any one of claims 13-15, further comprising one or more storage tanks for waste scrubber liquid located between said wet scrubber and said electrolytic cell. 17. The apparatus according to any one of claims 13-16, wherein said fuel is hydrogen. 18. The apparatus according to any one of claims 13-17, comprising; a. a wet scrubber, said wet scrubber comprising at least one gas inlet, at least one scrubber liquid inlet, at least one chamber in which gas from said gas inlet and scrubber liquid from said scrubber liquid inlet are mixed, and at least one outlet from said at least one chamber for waste scrubber liquid; b. an electrolytic cell arranged so as to receive waste scrubber liquid from said wet scrubber via said waste scrubber liquid outlet and subject it to electrolysis, said electrolytic cell comprising at least one electrolysis chamber, at least one cathode, at least one anode, at least one power supply connected to said at least one cathode and at least one anode, at least one fuel outlet and at least one concentrated waste scrubber liquid outlet. 19. The apparatus according to claims 18, wherein the scrubber liquid is seawater. 20. Use of the method according to any one of claims 1-12, or the apparatus according to any one of claims 13-19, in a power plant, waste disposal facility, ship or vehicle. 21. A power plant, waste disposal facility, ship or vehicle comprising the apparatus according to any one of claims 13-19.
